claims.text: A magnitude 7.8 earthquake struck at 5.5996, 125.0647 (offshore Sarangani, Philippines) on 7 June 2026 at 23:37 UTC, at 35 km depth, generating a maximum tsunami wave of 1.93 metres; GDACS issued an orange alert for medium humanitarian impact with about 2.9 million people exposed to Modified Mercalli Intensity VII or greater.

At least 19 people were killed, 12 remain missing and at least 134 were injured after the 7.8 magnitude earthquake struck Sarangani province on 8 June 2026, according to OCHA, with authorities noting the figures were provisional as assessments continued; more than 3.2 million learners were affected with classes suspended in over 6,200 schools.

By 23 June, government figures reported 77 deaths, with about 1.6 million people affected across Regions IX, XI, XII, and BARMM. PHIVOLCS recorded more than 9,400 aftershocks and further seismic activity expected. More than 80,000 houses have been damaged, including nearly 15,000 destroyed, with 884 damaged infrastructure losses and 130 affected roads and 47 bridges limiting access to isolated communities. Eighteen local government units (LGUs) have declared a state of calamity.

DSWD DROMIC Report #58, as of 09 July 2026, 6AM, reports 391,891 families (1,655,313 persons) affected in 650 barangays across Regions IX, XI, XII and BARMM, with 24,097 families (92,256 persons) still displaced — 7,008 families (28,090 persons) inside 74 evacuation centers and 17,089 families (64,166 persons) staying outside evacuation centers with relatives or friends. A total of 121,426 houses were damaged, of which 19,305 were totally destroyed and 102,121 partially damaged.

DSWD DROMIC Report #60, as of 10 July 2026, 6AM, reports 391,871 families (1,655,313 persons) affected in 650 barangays across Regions IX, XI, XII and BARMM, with 24,919 families (95,129 persons) still displaced — 7,830 families (30,963 persons) inside 79 evacuation centers and 17,089 families (64,166 persons) staying outside evacuation centers with relatives or friends. A total of 121,307 houses were damaged, of which 19,188 were totally destroyed and 102,119 partially damaged.

DSWD DROMIC Report #64, as of 13 July 2026, 6PM, reports a total of 391,891 families (1,655,313 persons) affected in 650 barangays in Regions IX, XI, XII and BARMM. There are 25,048 families (95,629 persons) still displaced in Regions XI, XII and BARMM — 7,959 families (31,463 persons) inside 80 evacuation centers in Regions XI and XII, and 17,089 families (64,166 persons) temporarily staying with relatives or friends. A total of 121,426 houses were damaged, of which 19,305 were totally damaged and 102,121 were partially damaged. A total of ₱1,156,615,547.45 worth of assistance has been provided to affected families, of which ₱1,156,595,547.45 came from DSWD and ₱20,000.00 from LGUs.

DSWD DROMIC Report #66, as of 16 July 2026, 6PM, reports a total of 391,975 families (1,655,686 persons) affected across 651 barangays across Regions IX, XI, XII and BARMM. There are 24,472 families (93,971 persons) still displaced — 7,383 families (29,805 persons) inside 78 evacuation centers and 17,089 families (64,166 persons) staying outside evacuation centers with relatives or friends. A total of 121,362 houses were damaged, of which 19,193 were totally destroyed and 102,169 partially damaged.

DSWD DROMIC Report #71, as of 20 July 2026, 6AM, reports a total of 391,975 families (1,655,686 persons) affected across 651 barangays across Regions IX, XI, XII and BARMM. There are 24,707 families (94,894 persons) still displaced — 7,618 families (30,728 persons) inside 78 evacuation centers and 17,089 families (64,166 persons) staying outside evacuation centers with relatives or friends. A total of 121,362 houses were damaged, of which 19,193 were totally destroyed and 102,169 partially damaged. Total cost of assistance provided to date is ₱1,284,626,667.47.

DSWD DROMIC Report #72, as of 20 July 2026, 6PM, reports 391,975 families (1,655,686 persons) affected across 651 barangays in Regions IX, XI, XII and BARMM (unchanged from Report #71). Total displaced: 24,640 families (94,559 persons) — 7,551 families (30,393 persons) inside 75 evacuation centers, 17,089 families (64,166 persons) outside with relatives/friends. Houses damaged: 121,362 total (19,193 totally damaged, 102,169 partially damaged) — same totals as Report #71. Cost of assistance provided to date: PHP 1,284,768,965.47.

description: A magnitude 7.8 earthquake struck offshore Sarangani province in the southern Philippines on 8 June 2026 (7:37am local time / 7 June 23:37 UTC), triggering a tsunami of nearly 2 metres and widespread damage across Mindanao. GDACS issued an orange alert for medium humanitarian impact. The confirmed death toll and number of affected people climbed through late June as damage assessments continued, and eighteen local government units have declared a state of calamity. As of 20 July 2026, displacement has eased only slightly, with about 94,500 people still displaced across evacuation centers and host families, even as housing-damage totals have largely leveled off.

impact.summary: As of DSWD DROMIC Report #72 (20 July 2026, 6PM), 391,975 families (1,655,686 persons) remain recorded as affected across 651 barangays in Regions IX, XI, XII and BARMM — unchanged from Report #71. Displacement has eased only marginally to 24,640 families (94,559 persons), down from 24,707 families (94,894 persons) at Report #71: 7,551 families (30,393 persons) remain in 75 evacuation centers and 17,089 families (64,166 persons) are staying with relatives or friends. A total of 121,362 houses have been damaged, including 19,193 totally destroyed and 102,169 partially damaged, unchanged from Report #71. Government figures still report 77 deaths, 1,339 injured and 31 missing (OCHA, as of 23 June — the latest allowlisted-source casualty figure; no OCHA narrative update has superseded it despite newer DROMIC reports). GDACS assessed an orange alert (medium humanitarian impact) with about 2.9 million people exposed to severe shaking and a tsunami of up to 1.93 metres; eighteen local government units have declared a state of calamity.

location.region: Offshore Sarangani province, Mindanao (near General Santos)

name: Mindanao M7.8 Earthquake & Tsunami — Philippines
